methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $80M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $5.0B.

data.narrative:
Sublime Systems is a Series B-stage climate and energy company founded by Leah Ellis and Yet-Ming Chiang that has raised $80M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of nine companies—one public (LNZA) and eight private—operating in the same sector and stage. Primary comparables include Commonwealth Fusion Systems ($5.0B), Pano AI ($500M), Sage Geosystems ($400M), Reverion ($310M), and CarbonCapture Inc. ($300M).

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using the $80M total raised as a proxy for implied pre-money value, applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yielding $152M. Peer-median valuation across the six-company same-sector, same-stage cohort produces $355M, with the peer set ranging from $120M to $5.0B. The wide dispersion reflects heterogeneous risk profiles and technology maturity within frontier climate tech.

Synthesizing both methods produces a triangulated range of $129M (low) to $408M (high), with a median of $254M. The gap between stage-progression and peer-median reflects uncertainty around capital intensity, commercialization timeline, and the degree to which Sublime's technology and market position align with higher-valued peers versus the broader cohort. Key sensitivities include pathway to near-term revenue, regulatory tailwinds or headwinds in decarbonization policy, and competitive positioning within cement/materials decarbonization.
